translations.en: So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) awoke from his sleep weeping. He came to his household, informed them of the dream, and bade them farewell. He loaded his sisters, his daughters, and his nephew al-Qasim b. al-Hasan b. 'Ali (peace be upon them) onto the howdahs, then set out with twenty-one men from his companions and his Ahl al-Bayt, among them: Abu Bakr b. 'Ali, Muhammad b. 'Ali, 'Uthman b. 'Ali, al-'Abbas b. 'Ali, 'Abdullah b. Muslim b. 'Aqil, 'Ali b. al-Husayn al-Akbar, and 'Ali b. al-Husayn al-Asghar. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar heard of his departure, mounted his mount, and set out hurrying behind him. He caught up with him at one of the stops and said: 'Where are you going, O son of the Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'Iraq.' He said: 'Slow down. Return to the sanctuary of your grandfather.' Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) refused. When Ibn 'Umar saw his refusal, he said: 'O Abu 'Abdillah! Show me the place where the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) used to kiss you.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) uncovered his navel, and Ibn 'Umar kissed it three times and wept, saying: 'I entrust you to Allah, O Abu 'Abdillah, for you will be killed on this journey of yours.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) and his companions set out. When they stopped at al-Tha'labiyya, a man named Bishr b. Ghalib came to him and said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! Inform me about the word of Allah: "That Day We shall summon every people with their Imam" [17:71].' He said: 'An Imam who calls to guidance, and they answer him — and an Imam who calls to misguidance, and they answer him. These are in Paradise, and those are in the Fire. That is His saying: "A party in Paradise and a party in the Blaze" [42:7].' Then he set out until he stopped at al-'Athib. He rested there at midday, then awoke from his rest weeping.
